message:
franken_numpy: ma masked_all_like wrapper + ma reality-check follow-ups

Land the masked_all_like ma helper (closing one of three ma reality-check
follow-ups), file the other two follow-ups as new beads, and refresh the
generated polynomial compliance dashboard now that the ifm0/nys5/ur9y
polynomial sweep that landed in this batch has driven that family to
100%.

crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs (franken_numpy-ldvv):

- New pyfunction masked_all_like(arr) that imports numpy at call time
  and forwards to numpy.ma.masked_all_like. Returns a fully-masked
  array shaped/dtyped like `arr`, byte-for-byte compatible with
  NumPy's masked-array shape/dtype propagation contract.
- Register masked_all_like on the module right after masked_all.
- Add masked_all_like to the nested ma alias list (around the
  "masked_all", "compressed", "getmask", ... block) so users can
  call it via fnp_python.ma.masked_all_like as well.
- Add an `assert!(module.getattr("masked_all_like").is_ok())` line in
  the registration smoke test so the new entry is exercised at test
  time.

crates/fnp-python/POLYNOMIAL_COMPLIANCE.generated.md:

- Regen reflecting the ifm0 (power-basis), nys5 (basis conversion),
  and ur9y (orthogonal Vandermonde) wrappers that landed earlier in
  this batch:
  - SHOULD coverage flips from 17/34 (50.0%) to 34/34 (100.0%).
  - Total coverage flips from 83/100 (83.0%) to 100/100 (100.0%).
  - chebyshev / hermite / hermite_e / laguerre / legendre / polynomial
    subtrees all flip to 100% — every previously-missing entry has
    landed.
  - "Missing names" table is now empty and the previously-listed
    17 entries (cheb2poly, chebvander, herm2poly, hermevander,
    hermvander, lag2poly, lagvander, leg2poly, legvander, poly2cheb,
    poly2herm, poly2lag, poly2leg, polyline, polytrim,
    polyvalfromroots, polyvander) all move into the "Present names"
    table with the right SHOULD level and subtree assignment.

.beads/issues.jsonl — three new ma reality-check beads:

- franken_numpy-ldvv (in_progress, assignee cod-numpy): "PORT ma:
  masked_all_like helper parity". Description records the reality-
  check evidence (numpy.ma.__all__ exposes masked_all_like but
  fnp_python.ma exposes only masked_all) and acceptance: passthrough
  wrapper with nested ma alias and oracle tests for masked-array
  shape/dtype propagation, plain ndarray input, and scalar/error
  surfaces. Marked in_progress here since the lib.rs wrapper above
  satisfies the implementation half of the bead.
- franken_numpy-jhbl (open): "PORT ma: mask row/column helper
  parity". Documents the missing mask_cols / mask_rows / mask_rowcols
  family with acceptance criteria for 2-D partially/full-masked
  arrays, axis behavior, and plain ndarray inputs.
- franken_numpy-742c (open): "PORT ma: notmasked/clump locator helper
  parity". Captures the remaining ma.__all__ gap: notmasked_edges,
  notmasked_contiguous, flatnotmasked_edges, flatnotmasked_contiguous,
  clump_masked, clump_unmasked. Acceptance: wrappers/nested aliases
  + oracle tests for 1-D / 2-D masks, all-masked, all-valid, and
  empty cases.
